name: Wild Mushroom and Oyster Bisque
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small dish, cover mushrooms with half a cup of boiling water and soak for 10 minutes.
2. In a saucepan, heat milk and cream over medium-low heat until very hot, but not simmering. Add roasted pepper, butter, vermouth or sherry, bay leaf, salt, Tabasco sauce, and nutmeg. Stir in fresh oysters and their liquor. If using canned oysters, rinse with cold water, drain, and add. Cover and cook until the edges of fresh oysters just start to curl or canned oysters are heated through, about 3 minutes. Do not boil.
3. Remove bay leaf, sprinkle with green onions, and serve.
Note: Fresh oysters will yield about 4 cups. If using canned oysters, use 15 ounces. To make ahead, prepare the soup without adding oysters or their liquor. Refrigerate for up to one day. Just before serving, reheat the soup, then stir in oysters and their liquor, and heat as above.
Per Serving:
- 210 calories
- 11.8 grams fat
- 201mg calcium
